finding: `node atm.mjs git commit --auto-stage --defer-foreign-staged` can spend several minutes inside pre-commit validation even after the task has already recorded fresh focused evidence. This is distinct from `ATM-BUG-2026-07-09-064` observability and `ATM-BUG-2026-07-09-065` task-card validator selection: the wrapper itself still lacks a fast path, validator-result reuse, or phase-level cache for the commit hook.
expectedBehavior: Governed commit should reuse fresh task evidence or a trusted validator cache when the commit bundle matches the already-validated scope, and it should expose phase timing so operators can see which hook step is consuming time. A normal atomic task commit should not spend four minutes rerunning heavyweight checks unless the touched paths or policy truly require it.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by the validator runner's manifest/receipt cache and status surface plus commit attempt status. Validator runs now record receipts, support resume/status polling, bound individual validator attempts with `--validator-timeout-ms`, and preserve a durable commit-attempt status packet so repeated heavyweight validation no longer appears as an opaque hung commit. Verification: `node --strip-types tests/cli/validator-run-resume-and-status.test.ts` and `node --strip-types tests/cli/git-commit-timeout-and-status.test.ts` pass.
followUp: Hook-level evidence reuse remains conservative for protected git-head provenance; broader skip policies should continue to be explicit and cache-keyed.
